headlines.title: Supreme Court allows states to count mail-in ballots that arrive late, rejecting RNC challenge
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — Rejecting a Republican National Committee challenge, the Supreme Court ruled Monday that elections officials may count mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day if they were postmarked beforehand

headlines.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s push to overturn $5 million E. Jean Carroll verdict
headlines.description: The Supreme Court refused to hear President Donald Trump’s attempt to overturn a jury's finding that he sexually assaulted and defamed writer E. Jean Carroll.

headlines.title: Supreme Court to weigh Arizona’s proof-of-citizenship voting law
headlines.description: The Supreme Court will consider whether Arizona rules requiring voters to show proof of citizenship when registering to vote violate federal law.

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran set for new talks, Trump says, after Hormuz clashes threatened peace deal
headlines.description: JERUSALEM — The United States and Iran will hold new talks on Tuesday in Qatar, President Donald Trump said Monday, after the two sides appeared to step back from a wave of attacks that threatened to derail peace efforts.“IRAN HAS REQUESTED A MEETING.

headlines.title: As nation turns 250, many Americans say the Stars and Stripes is now a red flag
headlines.description: The American flag can have many meanings, whether hung on front porches or waved in parades and protests. To some, it’s also tied up with President Trump.

headlines.title: Trump Taps Oklahoma State Trooper as Next ICE Director
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced he is nominating former Oklahoma State Trooper Lance Schroyer to lead ICE as its next director. Schroyer is a close ally of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin. It comes as Trump spent the weekend touring sites of his current and future renovations of the nation’s capital. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: US and Iran Pause Hostilities After Weekend of Escalating Strikes
headlines.description: The United States and Iran appear to have paused hostilities following a weekend of escalating attacks that rocked the Middle East, less than a week after Vice President JD Vance announced both sides had set a foundation for a lasting peace deal. Now, a new round of peace talks appears to be on the table as the war enters its fifth month. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Remembering Alan Greenspan and Andrea Mitchell's joint Meet the Press interview (2007)
headlines.description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan joined Meet the Press with his wife, NBC News chief foreign affairs correspondent Andrea Mitchell, in 2007. Greenspan passed away on June 22, 2026, at 100 years old.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: How an under-the-radar GOP official is shaping local voting plans in North Carolina
headlines.description: After Republicans shifted key election authorities to State Auditor Dave Boliek, Democrats and voting rights activists have criticized his involvement in early voting site discussions.

headlines.title: Great American State Fair attendees say they haven’t noticed politics overshadowing the event
headlines.description: Trump kicked off the event with a headlining speech Wednesday after musical acts dropped out of the lineup citing concerns over partisanship.

headlines.title: Trump’s ‘big, beautiful bill’ is bringing a big set of student loan changes
headlines.description: Starting Wednesday, many student borrowers may tackle higher repayment requirements, fewer program options and greater loan limits.
